error_signature: parameter declared outside parameter port list must have a default value syntax error invalid module item
raw_error_example:
<FILE>:<N>: error: parameter declared outside parameter port list must have a default value.
<FILE>:<N>: syntax error
<FILE>:<N>: error: Invalid module item.
root_cause: A module-level `parameter` (declared inside the module body, not in the #(...) port list) was written without a default value, e.g. `parameter W;`. Icarus Verilog rejects this — all body-level parameters must have an explicit default.
fix_rule: Every `parameter` declared inside a module body must have `= <value>` in the same declaration. If the value is meant to be set externally, move it into the `#( ... )` parameter port list or add a sensible default like `= 16`.
